put.summary: Confirm a booking
put.description:
- set the booking to BOOKING_STATE_SOLD
- set the booking items to BOOKING_STATE_LINE_BOOKED or BOOKING_STATE_LINE_BOOKED_NOT_SOLD depending on the payments done
- lock the stocks

post.summary: Confirm a booking with payments
post.description:
- set the booking to BOOKING_STATE_SOLD
- set the booking items to BOOKING_STATE_LINE_BOOKED or BOOKING_STATE_LINE_BOOKED_NOT_SOLD depending on the payments done
- set the booking payments with the payments provided
- lock the stocks

put.summary: Update datas of a cash register
put.description:
Updates datas of a single cash register.
 ## Request body sample
 ```json
{
  "updated":
  {
    "name": "My first cash register",
    "phoneNumber": "+33 6 00 00 00 00",
    "mobilePhoneNumber": "",
    "whatsAppNumber": "+ 1 800 548 548",
    "hasQrCodeInReceipt": false,
    "saisiePaxParticipants" : true,
    "latitude": 48.11111111,
    "longitude": 3.11111111,
    "address": 
    {
       "streetName": "221B Baker Street",
       "addressAddOn": "North London",
       "cedex": "BP 125",
       "zipCode": "NW1 6XE",
       "cityLabel": "London",
       "countryLabel": "Great Britain"
    }
  }
}
 ```

200.description: Success. Cash register data correctly updated.

The following properties can be expanded in the response:
- **Translations**: List of translations associated with the OI.

- **Photos**: List of photos associated with the OI.
- **Descriptions**: Descriptions of the OI.
- **Documents**: List of documents associated with the OI.
- **Links**: External links related to the OI.

- **OfficialRating**: Official rating of the OI (if available).
- **Labels**: Labels associated with the OI.
- **Themes**: Themes associated with the OI.
- **PaymentModes**: Payment modes accepted by the OI.
- **Tags**: Tags associated with the OI.
- **Cgvs**: General terms and conditions (CGV) of the OI.
- **Contacts**: Contacts associated with the OI.

post.summary: Creates an access token.
post.description:
Creates an access token for the provided credentials. An access token is returned along a refresh token. The access token has a short lifetime (minutes or hours) whereas the refresh token has a long lifetime (days).
            The refresh token is used to get another access token, along with the original (expired or not) access token without having to provide any credentials.
            Expiry time for the access and refresh tokens are returned in the response.

requestBody.description: The credentials used for the access token creation.

 ```json
{
    "idPrestation": "A1321B73-DE36-4000-83F0-280F7100B6D5",
    "order": 1,
    "idPrestationUpsell": "762E603B-A5E3-4F17-8207-C079F5183B38",
    "discountValue": 8.5,
    "isDiscountPercentage": true,
    "isDiscountRounded": true,
    "dateValidFrom": "2023-04-04T08:00:00.000",
    "dateValidTo": "2023-04-04T08:00:00.000",
    "datePublishedFrom": "2022-08-24T14:15:22Z",
    "datePublishedTo": "2022-08-24T14:15:22Z",
    "isBookingTime": true,
    "isArrivalTime": true,
    "isEmail": true,
    translations
     {
         "incitativeText": "Bonjour.",
         "idLang": 134
     },
     {
         "incitativeText": "Hello.",
         "idLang": 20
     }
}
 ```

listing_expectations_for_guests.description: Details guests must know about the home. Set expectations that the guests must agree to before booking.

cancellation_policy_category.description:
Cancellation policy for the listings. API only supports some of Airbnb's cancellation policies (https://developer.airbnb.com/v2023.06.30/reference/enumeration-values#cancellation-policies).
better_strict_with_grace_period is displayed as Firm on Airbnb
 possible values are : flexible, moderate, strict, better_strict_with_grace_period, strict_14_with_grace_period, super_strict_30, super_strict_60, flexible_new, moderate_new, strict_new, super_strict_30_new, super_strict_60_new

lts_cancellation_policy_id.description:
Cancellation policy - Long Term for the listings. API supports two of Airbnb's cancellation policies - long term. (https://developer.airbnb.com/v2023.06.30/reference/enumeration-values#long-term-cancellation-policies)
CANCEL_LONG_TERM_FAIR is displayed as Firm on Airbnb
CANCEL_LONG_TERM_WITH_GRACE_PERIOD is displayed as Strict on Airbnb
Setting a Long Term Cancellation policy is not supported for Listings in Italy

non_refundable_price_factor.description:
Non-refundable discount. If set, multiply the price by this factor to offer guests a standard rate and a non-refundable rate. Must be between 0 and 1.
 If null or omitted, the guest cannot select a tiered pricing option.

locale.description: The locale of this response/translation. Must be a supported locale. (https://developer.airbnb.com/reference/supported-locales)

idLang.description:
Id of the language used. Added by elloha.
Mapped with locale, can be null. Several locale can have the same IdLang.

description.description:
The entire listing description. 
This field is not updatable and is aggregated from the following fields: space, access, interaction, neighborhood_overview, transit, and notes.

name.description:
If a name was specified using the Listings API, this name will overwrite it.
If a name is specified in future Listings API requests, this name will be overwritten too.Please do not update the name by using Listings API.
Otherwise, this name may be overwritten unexpectedly.

summary.description: Should cover the major features of the space and neighborhood in 500 characters or less.

space.description: what makes it unique, and how many people does it comfortably fit.

access.description: Information about what parts of the space the guests will be able to access.

interaction.description: How much the Host will interact with the guests, and if the Host will be present during the guest stay.

transit.description:
Information on getting to the property. 
Is there convenient public transit? Is parking included with the listing or nearby? How does the guest get to the listing from the airport?

notes.description: Any additional details for the guest to know.

house_rules.description: Instructions for guests on how to behave. Should also include whether pets are allowed and if there are rules about smoking.

error_reason.description: Optional enum value associated with error

type.description: The type of the restriction the guests must agree to. Must be one of Listing Expectation Types.

allows_children_as_host.description: Is listing suitable for children (2-12 years)?

allows_infants_as_host.description: Is listing suitable for infants (under 2 years)?

children_not_allowed_details.description: If you selected false for any of the above, this field is required to provide details about why the place is not suitable for children.

foreigner_eligible_status_as_host.description:
This field is only applicable to some listings within China.
Are you eligible to host foreigner(non-citizen) guests? The default is yes(0). Specify 1 only if you can not.

name.description:
Listing name. Must be 1-50 characters. 
If you already use Listing Description API to specify the name, please do not update name by using this Listings API. 
Otherwise, the name may be overwritten unexpectedly.

property_type_group.description: Generalized group of the property types.

room_type_category.description: Generalized group of the property types.

bathrooms.description: Number of bathrooms. A three-quarter bath can be listed as a full or half bath (1 or 0.5), but this should be explained in the details.

amenities.description: Map with key as amenity name from the list of supported amenities available in this listing.

has_availability.description: Whether the listing is listed or not.

street.description: Street address. Should include number, street name, and street suffix.

state.description: State, territory, district, or province. For US states use the official two-letter abbreviation.

country_code.description: Listing's two-letter country code, capitalized. Format: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2.

lat.description: Latitude. You can use Google Maps to find the listing's latitude and longitude.

metadata.description:
A map of string keys to string values that you can attach to a photo. 
At most 10 key-value pairs, with key names up to 40 characters long and values up to 500 characters long.

You can clear a key pair value by sending an empty string for the value, e.g. {"internal_photo_id": ""}.

GmbAttribute.description:
A location attribute. Attributes provide additional information about a location. 
The attributes that can be set on a location may vary based on the properties of that location (for example, category). 
Available attributes are determined by Google and may be added and removed without API changes.

additionalCategories.description:
Optional. Additional categories to describe your business. 
Categories help your customers find accurate, specific results for services they're interested in. 
To keep your business information accurate and live, make sure that you use as few categories as possible to describe your overall core business. 
Choose categories that are as specific as possible, but representative of your main business.

GmbCategories.description:
A collection of categories that describes the business. During updates, both fields must be set. 
Clients are prohibited from individually updating the primary or additional categories using the update mask.

GmbCategory.description: A category describing what this business is (not what it does). For a list of valid category IDs, and the mappings to their human-readable names, see categories.list.

GmbLatlng.description:
An object that represents a latitude/longitude pair. 
This is expressed as a pair of doubles to represent degrees latitude and degrees longitude. 
Unless specified otherwise, this object must conform to the WGS84 standard. 
Values must be within normalized ranges.

storeCode.description:
External identifier for this location, which must be unique within a given account. 
This is a means of associating the location with your own records.

title.description:
Location name should reflect your business's real-world name, as used consistently on your storefront, website, and stationery, and as known to customers. 
Any additional information, when relevant, can be included in other fields of the resource (for example, Address, Categories). 
Don't add unnecessary information to your name (for example, prefer "Google" over "Google Inc. - Mountain View Corporate Headquarters"). 
Don't include marketing taglines, store codes, special characters, hours or closed/open status, phone numbers, website URLs, service/product information, location/address or directions, or containment information (for example, "Chase ATM in Duane Reade").

GmbPhoneNumbers.description:
A collection of phone numbers for the business. 
During updates, both fields must be set. 
Clients may not update just the primary or additional phone numbers using the update mask. 
International phone format is preferred, such as "+1 415 555 0132", see more in (https://developers.google.com/style/phone-numbers#international-phone-numbers).

revision.description: The schema revision of the PostalAddress. This must be set to 0, which is the latest revision.

regionCode.description:
Required. CLDR region code of the country/region of the address. 
This is never inferred and it is up to the user to ensure the value is correct. 
See https://cldr.unicode.org/ and https://www.unicode.org/cldr/charts/30/supplemental/territory_information.html for details. 
Example: "CH" for Switzerland.

languageCode.description:
Optional. BCP-47 language code of the contents of this address (if known). 
This is often the UI language of the input form or is expected to match one of the languages used in the address' country/region, or their transliterated equivalents. 
This can affect formatting in certain countries, but is not critical to the correctness of the data and will never affect any validation or other non-formatting related operations.
If this value is not known, it should be omitted (rather than specifying a possibly incorrect default)
Examples: "zh-Hant", "ja", "ja-Latn", "en".

postalCode.description:
Optional. Postal code of the address. 
Not all countries use or require postal codes to be present, but where they are used, 
they may trigger additional validation with other parts of the address (e.g. state/zip validation in the U.S.A.).

sortingCode.description:
Optional. Additional, country-specific, sorting code. 
This is not used in most regions. 
Where it is used, the value is either a string like "CEDEX", optionally followed by a number (e.g. "CEDEX 7"), 
or just a number alone, representing the "sector code" (Jamaica), "delivery area indicator" (Malawi) or "post office indicator" (e.g. Côte d'Ivoire).

administrativeArea.description:
Optional. Highest administrative subdivision which is used for postal addresses of a country or region. 
For example, this can be a state, a province, an oblast, or a prefecture. Specifically, for Spain this is the province and not the autonomous community (e.g. "Barcelona" and not "Catalonia"). 
Many countries don't use an administrative area in postal addresses. E.g. in Switzerland this should be left unpopulated.

locality.description:
Optional. Generally refers to the city/town portion of the address. Examples: US city, IT comune, UK post town. 
In regions of the world where localities are not well defined or do not fit into this structure well, leave locality empty and use addressLines.

sublocality.description: Optional. Sublocality of the address. For example, this can be neighborhoods, boroughs, districts.

addressLines.description:
Unstructured address lines describing the lower levels of an address.
Because values in addressLines do not have type information and may sometimes contain multiple values in a single field(e.g. "Austin, TX"), 
it is important that the line order is clear.The order of address lines should be "envelope order" for the country/region of the address.
In places where this can vary (e.g.Japan), address_language is used to make it explicit (e.g. "ja" for large-to-small ordering and "ja-Latn" or "en" for small-to-large). 
This way, the most specific line of an address can be selected based on the language.
The minimum permitted structural representation of an address consists of a regionCode with all remaining information placed in the addressLines.
It would be possible to format such an address very approximately without geocoding, but no semantic reasoning could be made about any of the address components until it was at least partially resolved.
Creating an address only containing a regionCode and addressLines, and then geocoding is the recommended way to handle completely unstructured addresses (as opposed to guessing which parts of the address should be localities or administrative areas).

recipients.description:
Optional. The recipient at the address. 
This field may, under certain circumstances, contain multiline information. For example, it might contain "care of" information.

organization.description: Optional. The name of the organization at the address.

GmbPostalAddress.description:
Represents a postal address, e.g. for postal delivery or payments addresses. 
Given a postal address, a postal service can deliver items to a premise, P.O. Box or similar. 
It is not intended to model geographical locations (roads, towns, mountains).

discountType.description: The discount type of the rate plan, if applicable

toDaysBeforeCheckin.description:
For EarlyBird discount types : 
The discount is valid for every booking made before {ToDaysBeforeCheckin} days before checkin

fromDaysBeforeCheckin.description:
For LastMinute discount types : 
The discount is valid for every booking made after {FromDaysBeforeCheckin} days before checkin.
Has to be combined with {FromHourBeforeCheckin} and {FromMinutesBeforeCheckin}
When the 3 fields {FromDaysBeforeCheckin}, {FromHourBeforeCheckin} and {FromMinutesBeforeCheckin} are combined :
Indicates that the discount is valid for every booking made after {FromDaysBeforeCheckin} days before checkin
from {FromHourBeforeCheckin}:{FromMinutesBeforeCheckin} hour

fromHourBeforeCheckin.description:
For LastMinute discount types : 
Indicates the hour from when the field {FromDaysBeforeCheckin} is valid.
Has to be combined with {FromDaysBeforeCheckin} and {FromMinutesBeforeCheckin}
When the 3 fields {FromDaysBeforeCheckin}, {FromHourBeforeCheckin} and {FromMinutesBeforeCheckin} are combined :
Indicates that the discount is valid for every booking made after {FromDaysBeforeCheckin} days before checkin
from {FromHourBeforeCheckin}:{FromMinutesBeforeCheckin} hour

fromMinutesBeforeCheckin.description:
For LastMinute discount types : 
Indicates the minutes from when the field {FromDaysBeforeCheckin} is valid.
Has to be combined with {FromDaysBeforeCheckin} and {FromHourBeforeCheckin}
When the 3 fields {FromDaysBeforeCheckin}, {FromHourBeforeCheckin} and {FromMinutesBeforeCheckin} are combined :
Indicates that the discount is valid for every booking made after {FromDaysBeforeCheckin} days before checkin
from {FromHourBeforeCheckin}:{FromMinutesBeforeCheckin} hour

nbDaysBeforeCheckinMax.description:

This rate plan can't be used on days before (checkin date - NbDaysBeforeCheckinMax)

hourBeforeCheckin.description: Indicates the hour limit to use the rate plan for a checkin on the day of the reservation
